name: TAO Retest Hold Derisk v1
edge_mechanism: retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether continuation after retest can remain viable if risk is reduced quickly when expected expansion does not appear. The edge is not just the retest hold; it is the time-based de-risking that prevents dead holds from decaying into churn.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest holds remain too sparse or if time-based de-risking cuts good trades without improving protection against failed expansions.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks, retests the broken structure, holds, and either expands promptly or stalls. The trade should survive only if expansion appears quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Retest continuation can work on volatile assets, but many holds fail because traders wait too long for expansion. Early de-risking can preserve the edge by cutting dead time.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best lane because retests and subsequent expansion bursts are meaningful, making time-based de-risking testable.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Either retests are too sparse, or the early de-risking exits too many good trades before expansion develops.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are adequate but time de-risking is too aggressive, extend the de-risk window before changing the retest logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A smaller set of continuation trades than mean reversion ideas, but with improved loss control when expansion never comes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cross-lane show real density and better protection from dead retests without killing too many winners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or de-risking that cuts expectancy more than it protects it.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest, hold, and time-based de-risking are coherent
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: improved relative to breakout-chase logic because entry waits for retest rather than rare breakout perfection

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing retest-hold continuation with fast de-risking on a volatile asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest holds occur often enough and early de-risking improves net outcomes after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Retests are too sparse or de-risking is unhelpful.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Held retests where quick expansion winners survive and dead holds are cut early.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same retest-and-derisk idea works on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the logic with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only works on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner retest holds than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same logic survives on a faster lane inside the allowed basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 1h keeps density without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller retest holds with quicker resolution.

long: If open after 2 bars without 0.35 * ATR_14 progress, cut 30% size
short: If open after 2 bars without 0.35 * ATR_14 progress, cut 30% size
